validation.outlierDescription: An outlier is a value that deviates significantly from other observations. These values may indicate data entry errors, interesting exceptional cases, or data issues.

iqr.name: IQR (Interquartile Range)
iqr.description: Robust method based on quartiles. Identifies values outside the 'normal' zone defined by the interquartile range.
iqr.details: Q1 = 25th percentile, Q3 = 75th percentile, IQR = Q3 - Q1. A value is considered an outlier if < Q1 - 1.5×IQR or > Q3 + 1.5×IQR.
iqr.bestFor: Asymmetric data or non-normal distributions. Standard in exploratory statistics.

zscore.name: Z-Score (Standard deviation)
zscore.description: Measures how many standard deviations a value is from the mean. Assumes normal distribution.
zscore.details: Z = (value - mean) / std deviation. A value with |Z| > 3 is considered an outlier (> 3 standard deviations from mean).
zscore.bestFor: Data following a normal (Gaussian) distribution. Physical measurements, heights, weights.
zscore.sensitivity: Low - only detects very extreme values

percentile.name: Percentile (1% - 99%)
percentile.description: Simply identifies the lowest 1% and highest 1% of values.
percentile.details: Any value below the 1st percentile or above the 99th percentile is an outlier. Simple and intuitive.
percentile.bestFor: When you want to identify a fixed percentage of extreme values, regardless of distribution.
percentile.sensitivity: Fixed - always detects exactly 2% of data (if present)

validation.valuesOutOfBounds: Values out of bounds according to {{method}} method

thresholdHints.percentile: 5% = excludes 5% extremes on each side (10% total)

geoCoverage.spatialAnalysisDescription: Run a spatial analysis to check the coverage of occurrences by imported shapes (uses geometries, may take time).
